title: Helical guidewires and related systems
abstract: In accordance with some implementations, various embodiments of a guidewire or catheter having an elongate core with a helical section are provided. In accordance with one implementation, a guidewire is provided. The guidewire includes a core wire having a proximal end, a distal end and is defined by an outer surface between the proximal end and the distal end of the core wire. The core wire has a centerline that traverses the length of the core wire from the proximal end to the distal end of the core wire. The centerline is located approximately at the center of mass along the core wire within the outer surface of the core wire. Preferably, the core wire has a substantially straight proximal portion and a helically curved segment located distally with respect to the proximal portion. The centerline of the core wire is substantially straight along the straight proximal portion, and, the centerline is helically curved along the helically curved segment. The disclosure similarly provides a catheter having the aforementioned attributes.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claim is: 1. A guidewire, comprising a core wire having a proximal end, and a distal end and being defined by an outer surface between the proximal end and the distal end of the core wire, said core wire having a centerline that traverses the length of the core wire from the proximal end to the distal end of the core wire, said centerline being located approximately at the center of mass along said core wire within said outer surface of said core wire, said core wire having a substantially straight proximal portion and a helically curved segment located distally with respect to said proximal portion, wherein the centerline of the core wire is substantially straight along said straight proximal portion and further wherein said centerline is helically curved along said helically curved segment. 2. The guidewire of Claim l, further comprising an outer tubular sleeve slidably disposed over said core wire. 3. The guidewire of Claim 2, wherein said outer tubular sleeve is configured and arranged to be slidably disposed over said helical segment of said core wire. 4. The guidewire of Claim 3, wherein slidably disposing said outer tubular sleeve over said helical segment of said core wire causes the helicity of said helical segment of said core wire to change. 5. The guidewire of Claim 4, wherein slidably disposing said outer tubular sleeve over said helical segment of said core wire causes said core wire to lose helicity. 6. The guidewire of Claim 4, wherein slidably disposing said outer tubular sleeve over said helical segment of said core wire causes said core wire to straighten. 7. The guidewire of Claim 1, wherein the helically curved segment is pre-formed into the core wire. 8. The guidewire of Claim 1, wherein the core wire is formed at least in part from a nickel -titanium shape memory material, and further wherein the helically curved segment is pre-formed into the core wire by a heat setting process. 9. The gui dewire of Claim 1, wherein a distal end region of the guidewire is helically shaped. 10. The guidewire of Claim 9, wherein the distal end region has a lower durometer than a proximal portion of the guidewire to permit the helically shaped distal end region of the guidewire to collapse axially when urged against tissue. 11. The guidewire of Claim 10, wherein a steering region of the guidewire proximal to the distal end region has a helical shape, wherein the steering region of the guidewire has a helicity with a longer pitch than the distal end region. 12. The guidewire of Claim 11, wherein the steering region of the guidewire is formed at least in part from a hypotube. 13. A delivery system for a cardiovascular prosthesis comprising a prosthesis disposed on a delivery catheter, and the guidewire of Claim 1 disposed through at least a portion of the length of the delivery catheter. 14. The delivery system of Claim 13, wherein the cardiovascular prosthesis includes a replacement heart valve. 15. The delivery system of Claim 14, wherein the replacement heart valve is configured and arranged to replace a native aortic valve. 16. The delivery system of Claim 14, wherein the replacement heart valve is configured and arranged to replace a native mitral valve. 17. The delivery system of Claim 14, wherein the replacement heart valve is configured and arranged to replace a native tricuspid valve. 18. The guidewire of Claim 4, wherein said outer tubular sleeve has a preformed curvature. 19. The guidewire of Claim 18, wherein the preformed curvature of the outer tubular sleeve is geometrically opposite to the curvature of helically curved segment of the core wire, and further wherein the guidewire is substantially straight when the outer tubular sleeve overlays the helically curved segment of the core wire. 20. The guide wire or delivery system of any preceding claim, wherein at least a portion of the core wire is surrounded by a coil that is attached to the core wire in at least one location. 21. A catheter, comprising an elongate core, said core having a proximal end, and a distal end and being defined by an outer surface between the proximal end and the distal end of the elongate core, said elongate core having a centerline that traverses the length of the elongate core from the proximal end to the distal end of the elongate core, said centerline being located approximately at the center of mass along said elongate core within said outer surface of said elongate core, said elongate core having a substantially straight proximal portion and a helically curved segment located distally with respect to said proximal portion, wherein the centerline of the elongate core is substantially straight along said straight proximal portion and further wherein said centerline is helically curved along said helically curved segment. 22. The catheter of Claim 21, further comprising an outer tubular sleeve slidably disposed over said elongate core. 23. The catheter of Claim 22, wherein said outer tubular sleeve is configured and arranged to be slidably disposed over said helical segment of said elongate core. 24. The catheter of Claim 23, wherein slidably disposing said outer tubular sleeve over said helical segment of said elongate core causes the helicity of said helical segment of said elongate core to change. 25. The catheter of Claim 24, wherein slidably disposing said outer tubular sleeve over said helical segment of said elongate core causes said elongate core to lose helicity. 26. The catheter of Claim 24, wherein slidably disposing said outer tubular sleeve over said helical segment of said elongate core causes said elongate core to straighten. 27. The catheter of Claim 21, wherein the helically curved segment is pre-formed into the elongate core. 28. The catheter of Claim 21, wherein the elongate core is formed at least in part from a nickel -titanium shape memory material, and further wherein the helically curved segment is pre-formed into the elongate core by a heat setting process. 29. The catheter of Claim 21, wherein the elongate core is tubular and defines a guidewire channel along at least a part of its length. 30. A method of delivering a prosthesis using a guidewire, catheter, or system according to any preceding claim comprising advancing a prosthesis into the region of a native heart valve, wherein the guidewire or catheter in the region of the native heart valve is at least partially helical, and adjusting the longitudinal or rotational position of the at least partially helical guidewire or catheter to adjust alignment of the prosthesis within the region of the native heart valve, and deploying the prosthesis into the region of the native heart valve. 31. A guidewire, comprising: a core wire having a proximal end, and a distal end; at least one tubular member wrapped at least partially around said core wire; and a tensionable tether disposed within a length of said at least one tubular member, wherein applying tension to said tensionable tether causes a curvature to be imparted to said core wire. 32. The guidewire of Claim 31, wherein said curvature is generally sinusoidal. 33. The guidewire of Claim 31, wherein said at least one tubular member is wrapped around said core wire in a generally helical pattern. 34. The guidewire of Claim 33, wherein said curvature is generally helical. 35. The guidewire of Claim 31, wherein said at least one tubular member is attached to said core wire. 36. The guidewire of Claim 35, wherein said at least one tubular member is attached to said core wire at a plurality of discrete locations along the length of said tubular member. 37. The guidewire of Claim 35, wherein said at least one tubular member is attached to said core wire continuously along the length of said tubular member. 38. The guidewire of Claim 31, wherein said at least one tubular member is directly attached to said core wire. 39. The guidewire of Claim 31, wherein said at least one tubular member is attached to a tubular coil disposed about said core wire. 40. The guidewire of Claim 31, further comprising a tubular coiled member surrounding at least a portion of said core wire. 41. The guidewire of claim 40, wherein said at least one tubular member is disposed between said core wire and said tubular coiled member. 42. The guidewire of Claim 31, wherein said at least one tubular member includes a plurality of tubular members disposed along a length of the guidewire, and further wherein said tensionable tether is disposed through at least one of said plurality of tubular members. 43. The guidewire of Claim 42, wherein said plurality of tubular members are disposed along the core wire in a pattern that resembles a helix. 44. The guidewire of Claim 31, wherein said tensionable tether is attached to said core wire proximate a distal end of the core wire. 45. The guidewire of Claim 31, wherein said tensionable tether is attached to said core wire at the distal end of the core wire. 46. The guidewire of Claim 31, wherein said tensionable tether is attached to said core wire at a location that is proximal to the distal end of the core wire. 47. The guidewire of Claim 31, further comprising an actuator located in a proximal region of the guidewire, said actuator being operably coupled to the tensionable tether, wherein applying force to the actuator applies tension to the tensionable tether causing the guidewire to deform into a helical shape. 48. The guidewire of Claim 31, further comprising a second tubular member wrapped at least partially around said core wire. 49. The guidewire of Claim 31, further comprising a second tubular member wrapped at least partially around said core wire, wherein each of said tubular members are helically wound around said core wire. 50. The guidewire of Claim 49, further comprising a second tensionable tether disposed within a length of said second tubular member and affixed to the core wire or second tubular member at a different axial location along the guidewire than the first tensionable tether, wherein applying tension to said second tensionable tether causes a different curvature to be imparted to said core wire than tensioning said first tensionable tether. 51. A guidewire or catheter, comprising: an elongate core having a proximal end, and a distal end; at least one strip of material wrapped helically about and attached to an exterior surface of said core, wherein windings of said strip of material are spaced apart from each other to define a helical groove; an outer tubular member disposed about said at least one strip and said elongate core, wherein an outer surface of said elongate core, said helical groove formed by said strip of material, and an inner surface of said outer tubular member define a helical channel between said elongate core and said outer tubular member; and a tensionable tether disposed within said helical channel affixed to a distal region of said guidewire and extending proximally toward a proximal end of said guidewire or catheter, wherein applying tension to said tensionable tether causes a curvature to be imparted to said catheter or guidewire. 52. The guidewire or catheter of Claim 51, wherein said curvature imparted to said catheter or guidewire is helical in shape. 53. A guidewire or catheter, comprising: an elongate core having a proximal end, and a distal end; a plurality of bosses or protrusions extending radially outwardly from an exterior surface of said elongate core, wherein said bosses or protrusions form a helical pattern around said exterior surface of said elongate core; an outer tubular member disposed about said elongate core, wherein said plurality of bosses are urged against an inner surface of said outer tubular member; and a tensionable tether disposed between said elongate core and said outer tubular member, wherein said tether is affixed to a distal region of said guidewire or catheter, and further wherein said tether is wound helically around said elongate core underneath said outer tubular member toward a proximal end of the guidewire or catheter, wherein applying tension to said tensionable tether causes the tether to pull against said bosses or protrusions and impart a curvature to said guidewire or catheter. 54. The guidewire or catheter of Claim 53, wherein said curvature imparted to said catheter or guidewire is helical in shape. 55. Said guidewire or catheter of Claim 53, wherein said outer tubular member is fused to said plurality of bosses or protrusions such that the elongate core and outer tubular member are an integral unit. 56. A guidewire or catheter, comprising: an elongate core having a proximal end, and a distal end, said elongate core having a helical groove formed along an exterior surface thereof along the length of said elongate core; an outer tubular member disposed about said elongate core, wherein an outer surface of said elongate core, said helical groove, and an inner surface of said outer tubular member define a helical channel between said elongate core and said outer tubular member; and 

 a tensionable tether disposed within said helical channel affixed to a distal region of said guidewire or catheter and extending proximally toward a proximal end of said guidewire or catheter, wherein applying tension to said tensionable tether causes a curvature to be imparted to said guidewire or catheter. 57. The guidewire or catheter of Claim 56, wherein said curvature imparted to said catheter or guidewire is helical in shape. 58. A guidewire, comprising: a core wire having a proximal end, and a distal end and being defined by an outer surface between the proximal end and the distal end of the core wire, said core wire having a centerline that traverses the length of the core wire from the proximal end to the distal end of the core wire, said centerline being located approximately at the center of mass along said core wire within said outer surface of said core wire, said core wire having a substantially straight proximal portion and a helically curved segment located distally with respect to said proximal portion, wherein the centerline of the core wire is substantially straight along said straight proximal portion and further wherein said centerline is helically curved along said helically curved segment; a tubular member helically wound around said helical segment of said core wire; and a tensionable tether disposed within said tubular member, wherein applying tension to said tensionable tether causes said guidewire or catheter to change in shape. 59. A guidewire or catheter, comprising: an elongate core having a proximal end, and a distal end and being defined by an outer surface between the proximal end and the distal end of the elongate core, said elongate core having a centerline that traverses the length of the elongate core from the proximal end to the distal end of the elongate core, said centerline being located approximately at the center of mass along said elongate core within said outer surface of said elongate core, said elongate core having a substantially straight proximal portion and a helically curved segment located distally with respect to said proximal portion, 

 wherein the centerline of the elongate core is substantially straight along said straight proximal portion and further wherein said centerline is helically curved along said helically curved segment; a tubular member helically wound around said helical segment of said elongate core; and a push rod disposed within said tubular member, wherein applying compression to said push rod causes said helically curved segment to straighten. 60. A guidewire or catheter, comprising: an elongate core having a proximal end, and a distal end and being defined by an outer surface between the proximal end and the distal end of the elongate core, said elongate core having a centerline that traverses the length of the elongate core from the proximal end to the distal end of the elongate core, said centerline being located approximately at the center of mass along said elongate core within said outer surface of said elongate core; a tubular member helically wound around said helical segment of said elongate core; and a push rod disposed within said tubular member, wherein applying compression to said push rod causes said guidewire or catheter to take on a helical curvature.
